content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Consider the sequence containing the integers 1,2,…,N1, 2, \dots, N1,2,…,N in increasing order (each exactly once). Find the length of the longest subarray in this sequence such that the bitwise AND of all elements in the subarray is **positive**.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single integer NNN on a single line.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the length of the longest subarray that satisfy the given property.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible subarray we can choose is [1][1][1].
Test case 222: We can't take the entire sequence [1,2][1, 2][1,2] as a subarray because the bitwise AND of 111 and 222 is zero. We can take either [1][1][1] or [2][2][2] as a subarray.
Test case 444: It is optimal to take the subarray [2,3][2, 3][2,3] and the bitwise AND of 222 and 333 is 222.
Test case 555: It is optimal to take the subarray [4,5,6,7][4, 5, 6, 7][4,5,6,7] and the bitwise AND of all integers in this subarray is 444.
